title: Who Really Owns the Platform Economy?
intro: 外卖、网约车、电商平台把买卖双方连在一起，但真正的价值和风险落在谁身上？
paragraphs: Digital platforms have become the invisible marketplaces of modern life. A single app can match a passenger with a driver, a shopper with a seller, or a freelancer with a client in seconds. Because the platform owns the connection rather than the goods, it can grow with remarkably little capital of its own.

This model creates enormous value, yet it also raises a difficult question of ownership. The workers who deliver the meals and the merchants who stock the shelves generate most of the activity, but the platform sets the rules, collects the data, and captures a large share of the profit.

Supporters insist that platforms simply lower the cost of doing business, opening opportunities to anyone with a phone and some spare time. Critics reply that flexible work often means unstable income and few protections, and that the convenience consumers enjoy is partly subsidized by those at the bottom.

As governments begin to regulate this sector, the central issue is balance: how to preserve the efficiency that platforms provide while ensuring that the people who make them work are treated fairly. The answer will shape the next decade of the economy.
cn: 数字平台已经成为现代生活中无形的市场。一个应用就能在几秒钟内为乘客匹配司机、为购物者匹配卖家、为自由职业者匹配客户。由于平台拥有的是连接而非商品本身，它能够用极少的自有资本实现增长。
这种模式创造了巨大的价值，却也提出了一个棘手的归属问题。送餐的工人和备货上架的商家创造了大部分活动，但制定规则、收集数据、并攫取大部分利润的，却是平台。
支持者坚称，平台只是降低了做生意的成本，为任何有一部手机和一些空闲时间的人打开了机会。批评者则回应说，灵活的工作往往意味着不稳定的收入和很少的保障，而消费者享受的便利，有一部分是由底层劳动者补贴的。
随着各国政府开始监管这一领域，核心问题在于平衡：如何在保留平台所带来的效率的同时，确保那些让平台运转的人得到公平对待。这个问题的答案，将塑造未来十年的经济。

questions.q: The phrase "invisible marketplaces" in Paragraph 1 suggests that platforms ___
options: connect buyers and sellers without owning the goods themselves
hide their profits from the public
are difficult for ordinary users to find
questions.explain: 语义理解题。第 1 段解释：平台拥有的是"连接"而非商品本身，市场功能隐藏在一个应用背后——invisible 指的是这种"看不见的中介"角色，不是违法或隐瞒。

questions.q: According to Paragraph 2, the question of ownership arises because ___
options: workers and merchants generate most activity while the platform captures much of the profit
platforms refuse to collect any data
workers set the rules of the platform
merchants own most of the platform's shares
questions.explain: 细节题。第 2 段：送餐工人和商家创造了大部分活动，但制定规则、收集数据、攫取大部分利润的是平台——干活的与得利的不一致，才引出"归属"之问。

questions.q: Critics of the platform economy would most likely agree that ___
options: anyone with a phone can easily become wealthy
platforms raise the cost of doing business
the convenience consumers enjoy is partly subsidized by those at the bottom
questions.explain: 观点题。第 3 段批评者认为：灵活工作往往意味着不稳定收入和缺乏保障，消费者的便利部分由底层劳动者补贴。A、B 是支持者口吻，C 与支持者"降低成本"说法相反。

questions.q: What does the author see as the central issue as governments begin to regulate platforms?
options: balancing platform efficiency with fair treatment of the people who make them work
slowing down the growth of the digital economy
questions.explain: 细节题。末段：核心问题在于平衡（balance）——既保留平台带来的效率，又确保让平台运转的人得到公平对待。

glossary.ex: to capture a share of the profit
